title: Asking Directions
description: Find your way, ask where things are, and understand simple directions in Japanese.
situation: Getting around: finding stations, landmarks, and shops, and following left, right, and straight-ahead directions.

phrases.native: すみません、道を教えてください
phrases.english: Excuse me, please show me the way

phrases.tip: Swap 駅 (eki, station) for any place you are looking for.

phrases.native: まっすぐ行ってください
右に曲がってください
phrases.tip: 左 (hidari) is left; just swap the word to ask for a left turn.

phrases.native: どのくらいかかりますか
地図を見せてください
phrases.english: Please show me on the map

phrases.native: この近くにコンビニはありますか
phrases.transliteration: kono chikaku ni konbini wa arimasu ka
phrases.english: Is there a convenience store near here?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-noh chee-kah-koo nee kon-bee-nee wah ah-ree-mahs kah

phrases.tip: A clear way to ask for help when you have no idea where you are.

phrases.english: Where am I (where is this place)?

phrases.native: ありがとうございました
phrases.english: Thank you very much (for the help)
phrases.tip: The past form ました thanks someone for help already given.
